problem: A wheel with radius $1\text{ m}$ is rolled in a straight line through one complete revolution on a flat horizontal surface. How many meters did the center of the wheel travel horizontally from its starting location?

classified_questions.question: Can you describe what happens to the circle as it rolls in a straight line through one complete revolution?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What point on the circle do we consider to determine the distance traveled by the center?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: If the circle makes one complete revolution, what is the distance traveled by the point of contact with the line?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we relate the distance traveled by the point of contact with the line to the distance traveled by the center of the wheel?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What shape can we create by completing a rectangle with the line and the points of contact on the circle?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How does the distance traveled by the center of the wheel relate to the circumference of the circle?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you calculate the distance traveled by the center of the wheel after one complete revolution?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: If the radius of the wheel was different, would the distance traveled by the center be the same? How would it change?
classified_questions.label: b
